skill.name: browser-extension-builder
skill.description: Guides users through browser extension design and implementation for Chrome, Firefox, and cross-browser projects, including Manifest V3, content scripts, storage, popup interfaces, and permission planning.
skill.summary: Build practical browser extensions with Manifest V3 architecture, content scripts, storage, and publishing guidance.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static command-execution, certificate, weak-crypto, reconnaissance, and combined critical heuristic findings are false positives caused by Markdown fences, placeholder text, and storage key examples. Two real documentation risks remain: the skill teaches direct innerHTML injection in a content script and starts with a broad <all_urls> content-script match, both of which need marketplace warning context.

medium_findings.title: Unsafe DOM Injection Pattern in Example
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: SKILL.md shows a content script assigning markup through innerHTML. The example uses static text, but browser extensions often combine page data and UI templates, so this pattern can lead users toward XSS-prone extension code.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The innerHTML assignment is real and appears inside content-script guidance. The immediate template is static, so exploitability is not confirmed, but the guidance pattern is risky.

medium_findings.title: Broad Host Access in Manifest Template
medium_findings.description: SEMANTIC_FINDING: The Manifest V3 example uses a content script match pattern for all URLs. Broad host access increases privacy risk and extension review friction, even though the skill later shows a narrower site-specific match.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The all-URLs match pattern is directly present in the manifest example. The risk is guidance-level because this is documentation, not executable package code.

low_findings.title: Markdown Fences Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The reported Ruby or shell backtick locations are Markdown code fences and documentation formatting. No evidence found of executable shell invocation, command interpolation, or runtime process execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are visible Markdown fences, diagrams, or inline skill names. There is no executable Ruby, shell, or command API in the skill file.

low_findings.title: Placeholder URL Misclassified as Network Behavior
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The hardcoded URL is a content-script match pattern for a placeholder website, not a network request. No evidence found of fetch, beacon, webhook, or data exfiltration behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is inside a manifest match-pattern example. It does not initiate network access or transmit data.

low_findings.title: Storage Key Examples Misclassified as Certificate Files
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The reported certificate or key findings refer to a storage field named key in Chrome storage examples. No evidence found of private keys, certificate files, or credential material.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is Chrome storage documentation and a generic property name. It is not a path, file extension, or secret value.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detector False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The reported weak-cryptography lines contain skill description text, a manifest description field, and permission advice. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or custom cryptographic logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual context review shows no cryptographic API or algorithm at those lines. The static category does not match the visible content.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Detector False Positive
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The reported reconnaissance line assigns a DOM element id for injected UI. No evidence found of host fingerprinting, OS checks, environment enumeration, or system information collection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed line is a constant DOM id assignment. It has no connection to local system reconnaissance.

low_findings.title: Combined Critical Heuristic Dismissed
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The critical heuristic combines dismissed command-execution, network, and credential signals with a real but limited innerHTML documentation pattern. No evidence found of malicious intent, credential access, obfuscation, or exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The underlying signals were reviewed in context and are mostly formatting or placeholder examples. The remaining concerns are documentation safety issues, not a confirmed malicious chain.

dangerous_patterns.title: Direct innerHTML Assignment in Content Script Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill demonstrates assigning markup with innerHTML inside a content script. Safer examples should prefer DOM construction or sanitized templates.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The direct assignment is present. The example does not include untrusted input, so the risk is educational rather than confirmed exploit code.

dangerous_patterns.title: All-URLs Content Script Match Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The manifest example uses <all_urls>, which can overexpose user browsing data if copied without narrowing scope.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The broad match pattern is explicitly shown. It is risky when used as a default extension template.

content.value_statement: Browser extension projects require careful choices about permissions, content scripts, storage, and browser policies. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured guidance for building practical Chrome and Firefox extensions.
actual_capabilities: Outlines a modern extension project structure with manifest, popup, content, background, options, and icon files.
Provides a Manifest V3 template with action, content script, background service worker, storage, and options page entries.
Explains content-script patterns for reading page content, responding to messages, and injecting a floating UI.
Shows Chrome storage usage for local storage, sync storage, change listeners, and async wrappers.
Lists extension anti-patterns involving excessive permissions, heavy background work, and fragile page selectors.
Covers browser extension topics such as cross-browser support, popup interfaces, monetization, and store publishing at a high level.
limitations: It is a guidance-only skill and does not include a runnable extension scaffold or build script.
Some examples are simplified and need security hardening before production use.
Publishing, monetization, and cross-browser guidance is high level rather than store-specific compliance advice.
The sample Manifest V3 template needs narrowed host permissions for most real extensions.

use_cases.title: Create a New Extension Plan
use_cases.description: Map the files, permissions, scripts, and storage model for a new browser extension before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Modernize Extension Architecture
use_cases.description: Convert an older extension concept into a Manifest V3 structure with a service worker and clearer content-script boundaries.

use_cases.title: Review Extension Scope
use_cases.description: Check whether permissions, background work, and page selectors are too broad or fragile before store submission.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Simple Extension
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me design a simple browser extension for this problem: [describe problem]. Include files, permissions, popup behavior, content scripts, storage needs, and risks.

prompt_templates.title: Draft Manifest V3 Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Manifest V3 architecture for a Chrome and Firefox extension that does [goal]. Explain the background worker, content scripts, popup, options page, and permission choices.

prompt_templates.title: Review Extension Security
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this browser extension plan for security and store review issues: [paste plan]. Focus on host permissions, content script data access, storage, injected UI, and background processing.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Store-Ready Scope
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me refine this extension for store submission: [paste feature list]. Identify minimum permissions, optional permissions, privacy disclosures, fragile selectors, and release-readiness gaps.

output_examples.input: I want an extension that highlights invoice numbers on a specific billing site.
output: A scoped extension architecture using one content script for the billing domain.
A permission plan that avoids all-sites access and requests only storage if settings are needed.
A popup and options outline for highlight color, enablement, and site-specific behavior.

output_examples.input: Review my extension idea before I build it.
output: A risk review covering host permissions, injected UI, background work, storage, and selector stability.
A list of changes that reduce store rejection risk and improve user trust.
A practical implementation sequence from prototype to submission.

output_examples.input: Help convert my extension to Manifest V3.
output: A migration outline that separates popup, content script, service worker, and options responsibilities.
Guidance for replacing persistent background behavior with event-driven service worker logic.
A checklist for cross-browser compatibility and release testing.

best_practices: Start with the narrowest host permissions and add optional permissions only when a feature needs them.
Treat page content as untrusted input and prefer safe DOM construction over raw HTML injection.
Keep background service workers small, event-driven, and resilient to Manifest V3 idle termination.
anti_patterns: Copying an all-sites content-script match pattern into production without a clear privacy reason.
Using direct HTML injection for user or page-derived data without sanitization.
Doing heavy continuous work in the background service worker instead of using events, alarms, or caching.

faq.question: Does this skill generate a complete browser extension project?
faq.answer: It provides architecture and implementation guidance. The repository contains documentation examples, not a complete scaffolded project.

faq.question: Can it help with Manifest V3?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes Manifest V3 structure, background service worker guidance, content scripts, popup files, options pages, and storage examples.

faq.question: Does it support Firefox add-ons?
faq.answer: The skill covers cross-browser extension concepts, but examples focus mainly on Chrome extension APIs and Manifest V3 patterns.

faq.question: Is the sample all-URLs permission safe to copy?
faq.answer: No. Use the narrowest site match that supports the feature, and request broader access only with a clear user benefit.

faq.question: Can it review extension security?
faq.answer: Yes. It can help review permissions, content-script behavior, storage, background processing, and injected UI patterns.

faq.question: Is it ready for marketplace publication?
faq.answer: It is publishable with a medium-risk warning because some examples need security hardening before production use.
